title: SLP PD Flock Safety Audit

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: SLP PD Flock Safety Audit
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) ALPR Audit Trail Logs (All Access & Activity)
Records comprising the ALPR data audit trail documenting all queries, searches, alerts, access, sharing, or dissemination events from 1/1/2025 through the date this request is processed. Per department policy, all such actions must be recorded in a data audit trail. Please include all available metadata, including but not limited to:
- Date and time
- User identifier (name or role)
- Query/search parameters
- Action taken (search, alert review, data access, sharing, etc.)

Please provide these records in a structured format (e.g., CSV, XLSX, or similar database format).
------
2) Access Justification / Purpose Logs
Records documenting the purpose, justification, or case association for each ALPR query or access event during the same time period. Department policy restricts access to legitimate law enforcement purposes tied to investigations or official actions. 
------
3) External Access / Inter-Agency Query Logs
Records of any access to SLP PD ALPR data by external agencies, including:
- Requesting agency
- Requestor identity (or role)
- Date/time
- Stated purpose
- Associated case number (if available)

Department policy requires written requests and documented justification for such access.
[Note - Common Flock usage logs may group 1, 2, and 3 together into one excel file. That is hopefully less work for you, and easier for us.] 
------
4) Manual Hot List Entries and Justifications
Records of all Manual Hot List entries during the requested time period, including:
- Plate entered
- Date of entry/removal
- Justification or associated investigation

Policy requires a “legitimate and specific law enforcement reason” for such entries.
------
5) Agreements with Private Entities (Flock / ALPR Access)
Any agreements, memoranda, or contracts with:

- Homeowner associations
- Private businesses
- Individuals
- Religious entities or groups

Relating to ALPR or Flock system deployment, access, or data sharing.
------
6) Electronic Communications with Flock Safety
Correspondence (emails, letters, memoranda) between SLP PD personnel and Flock Safety (domain: @flocksafety.com). Timeframe: 6/1/2025 to present
You may de-duplicate mass emails or newsletters.
------
7) Biennial ALPR Audit Reports and Submissions
Copies of all biennial ALPR audit reports, including any reports, summaries, or submissions provided to the Minnesota Commissioner of Administration, legislative committees, or other oversight bodies, for the past two years.
------

I realize this is a large request and appreciate you taking the time to complete it. Please feel free to respond in multiple parts as documents become available.

I have already reviewed: 
A)  Flock Transparency Page: https://transparency.flocksafety.com/saint-louis-park-mn-pd
B) SLP PD ALPR Policy: https://www.stlouisparkmn.gov/home/showpublisheddocument/28047/638681458304130000
C) Location of Devices: https://dps.mn.gov/divisions/bca/data-and-reports/agencies-use-lprs-lpr

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

communications.subject: RE: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: SLP PD Flock Safety Audit
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

We have received your data request. Given the large scope of this request, a pre-payment of $20.00 at a $0.25 per page fee rate will be required under MN Stat. 13.03 subd. 3(c). We will contact you should additional fees exists after all the reports are gathered or if any payment is due back to you. Please confirm you would like to continue this request or would like to narrow the scope of this request. We will begin responding to your request when pre-payment is received. Please note that after payment is received, because you are not the data subject of this request, the government must respond within a reasonable amount of time.

Please let me know if you would like to move forward with this data request. I will close this data request on March 30, 2026, following Minn. Stat. § 13.03, subd. 3(g) if I don’t receive a response.

Data requested:

1) ALPR Audit Trail Logs (All Access & Activity)
Records comprising the ALPR data audit trail documenting all queries, searches, alerts, access, sharing, or dissemination events from 1/1/2025 through the date this request is processed. Per department policy, all such actions must be recorded in a data audit trail. Please include all available metadata, including but not limited to:
- Date and time
- User identifier (name or role)
- Query/search parameters
- Action taken (search, alert review, data access, sharing, etc.)

Please provide these records in a structured format (e.g., CSV, XLSX, or similar database format).
------
2) Access Justification / Purpose Logs
Records documenting the purpose, justification, or case association for each ALPR query or access event during the same time period. Department policy restricts access to legitimate law enforcement purposes tied to investigations or official actions.
------
3) External Access / Inter-Agency Query Logs
Records of any access to SLP PD ALPR data by external agencies, including:
- Requesting agency
- Requestor identity (or role)
- Date/time
- Stated purpose
- Associated case number (if available)

Department policy requires written requests and documented justification for such access.
[Note - Common Flock usage logs may group 1, 2, and 3 together into one excel file. That is hopefully less work for you, and easier for us.]
------
4) Manual Hot List Entries and Justifications
Records of all Manual Hot List entries during the requested time period, including:
- Plate entered
- Date of entry/removal
- Justification or associated investigation

Policy requires a “legitimate and specific law enforcement reason” for such entries.
------
5) Agreements with Private Entities (Flock / ALPR Access)
Any agreements, memoranda, or contracts with:

- Homeowner associations
- Private businesses
- Individuals
- Religious entities or groups
Relating to ALPR or Flock system deployment, access, or data sharing.
------
6) Electronic Communications with Flock Safety
Correspondence (emails, letters, memoranda) between SLP PD personnel and Flock Safety (domain: @flocksafety.com). Timeframe: 6/1/2025 to present
You may de-duplicate mass emails or newsletters.
------
7) Biennial ALPR Audit Reports and Submissions
Copies of all biennial ALPR audit reports, including any reports, summaries, or submissions provided to the Minnesota Commissioner of Administration, legislative committees, or other oversight bodies, for the past two years.


Christine Johnson
Records Supervisor | City of St. Louis Park
3015 Raleigh Ave. S., St. Louis Park, MN 55416
Office: 952.924.2605 | Fax: 952.924.2676
cjohnson2@stlouisparkmn.gov<mailto:cjohnson2@stlouisparkmn.gov>
www.stlouisparkmn.gov<http://www.stlouispark.org/>
Experience LIFE in the Park.

communications.subject: RE: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: SLP PD Flock Safety Audit
communications.communication:
Hi Christine,

As a resident of SLP, it is disappointing that I have to pay money to my city to understand the type of surveillance technology being used in a moment where the public interest in this technology is at an all-time high. Given that there have been recent abuses of Flock Safety systems in Illinois and Wisconsin, I would hope that transparency and showcasing responsible use of these systems would be a priority for the city. 

To simplify my request, lets combine requests 1, 2, and 3 into the following two .csv (or .xls) files that are simple to download from the Flock system:
1. Organization Audit in Flock.
Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.
---

I am requesting that you waive the $20 fee due to the public interest in how SLP PD is sharing data about the movement of its residents with dozens of law enforcement agencies and task forces around the state. During similar audits, law enforcement agencies (e.g. Mountain View, CA) discovered that federal agencies were accessing local Flock Data without the city's knowledge and against city policy. If you still require the pre-payment, please let me know how to pay. 

Thank you,
AJ Segal

Links:
- Illinois - Illegal use of Flock for immigration cases: https://unraveledpress.com/a-dea-agent-used-an-illinois-police-officers-flock-license-plate-reader-password-for-unauthorized-immigration-enforcement-searches/
- Illinois - State says Flock broke the law: https://www.oakpark.com/2025/08/28/state-says-flock-safety-broke-the-law/
- Wisconsin - Misuse of Flock ALPR data for stalking: https://wisconsinexaminer.com/2026/02/26/milwaukee-officer-accused-of-misusing-flock-surveillance-cameras/
- California - Local department removes Flock after resident data is accessed by federal agencies: https://www.mountainview.gov/Home/Components/News/News/1211/284
- California - Privacy Violation Class Action Lawsuit: https://www.ktvu.com/news/class-action-lawsuit-alleges-flock-license-plate-readers-violate-ca-law-privacy-residents

communications.subject: RE: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: SLP PD Flock Safety Audit
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

We have received your amended request.

While I understand the frustration over fees for data, we are bound by MN. Stat. 13.03 and MN Rules 1205.0300, subpart 4.  The actual cost for this amended data request would be a calculation of the retrieval of data at an employee hourly rate. You can find more information about that here: link<https://mn.gov/admin/data-practices/data/rules/copy-costs/#:~:text=25%20cents%20per%20page%20for%20paper%20copies%20of%20100%20or%20fewer%20pages&text=This%20charge%20is%20a%20flat,cost%20of%20mailing%20or%20paper.>

I will forward your request for legal review should any protections exist under MN Stat. 13.82 subd. 7
I will connect with you on any actual costs that may exist when the legal review has been completed. Please note that because you are not the data subject of this request, the government must respond within a reasonable amount of time.


Data requested:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.


Christine Johnson
Records Supervisor | City of St. Louis Park
3015 Raleigh Ave. S., St. Louis Park, MN 55416
Office: 952.924.2605 | Fax: 952.924.2676
cjohnson2@stlouisparkmn.gov<mailto:cjohnson2@stlouisparkmn.gov>

communications.subject: Kentucky Open Records Act Request: Jeremy stinnett
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Kentucky Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

911 audio of the call placed by [Patricia G. Hook ] on [July 9, 2022 ] at approximately [7:40 p.m. to 7:45 p.m ], reporting [that kathy cann shot Jeremy stinnett  ] at [6600 block of Grayson Springs Road,  Grayson County, Kentucky].

Body-worn camera video and audio from the first-responding officer(s) at [6600 block of Grayson Springs Road,], documenting arrival and initial assessment of the scene (including confirmation of deceased victim(s), if applicable).


Body-worn camera video and audio capturing [the conversation with kathy cann ] at approximately [8:58pm ] on [July 9, 2022] at [6600 block of Grayson Springs Road,]

Body-worn camera video and audio capturing [Patricia describing the whole incident in the crime scene ] at approximately [9:21 pm] on July 9, 2022] at [6600 block of Grayson Springs Road,]

Full interview/interrogation room video and audio recording(s) of any custodial interview(s) or interrogation(s) of [Kathy Cann ] conducted by [Grayson County Sheriff’s Office ] on [July 9, 2022] 



Full interview/interrogation room video and audio recording(s) of any custodial interview(s) or interrogation(s) of [Patricia G. Hook ] conducted by [Grayson County Sheriff’s Office ] on [July 9, 2022] 


Full video and audio recording(s) of witness interview(s) conducted with [Jeremy stinnett (Victim of this case) ] conducted by the [Grayson County Sheriff’s Office ] on [19 July 2022].


Body-worn camera and/or dash/MVR video and audio from the primary arresting officer(s) documenting the arrest of [Eddie Cann ] on [19 July 2022] at [6600 block of Grayson Springs Road,] from first contact through handcuffing/detention, escort, and placement into the patrol vehicle (including any spontaneous statements captured during the arrest).


Full interview/interrogation room video and audio recording(s) of any custodial interview(s) or interrogation(s) of [Kathy Cann ] conducted by [Grayson County Sheriff’s Office ] on [July 19, 2022]


Full interview/interrogation room video and audio recording(s) of any custodial interview(s) or interrogation(s) of [Eddie Cann  ] conducted by [Grayson County Sheriff’s Office ] on [July 19, 2022]

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Danielle D.

title: Phone and text message records between police and ICE in Massachusetts (Fall River Police Department)

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Phone and text message records between police and ICE in Massachusetts (Fall River Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:  

1. All departmental or municipal policies regarding cell phone use and procedures for retaining cell phone and landline phone data. The records request includes, but is not limited to, policies regarding: 

*   Officer or employee use of cellphones 
*       Officer use of social media 
*       Records retention practices of phone logs and text messages 
*       Mobile device (i.e. cell phone) management 
*       Authorized software & internet usage on department-issued devices 
*       Bringing your own device 

This request covers any policies that are currently in effect or that have been in effect at any point on or after January 20, 2025. 

2. Current contract and most recent procurement records related to managing mobile phone devices within your department. This includes information about the mobile device management system (e.g. Microsoft Intune, Panasonic Connect, etc.)  that is used by the department to manage employee cell phone and computer communications, as well as screenshots of how department mobile technology is centrally managed. 

3. Call logs, if any, from January 20, 2025, to the day this request is responded to, between a department phone (including both department provided cell phones and landlines) and the following numbers: 

*   (802) 872-6020   
*   (781) 359-7500  
*   (860) 244-0522 
*   (413) 221-1303 
*   (857) 416-3742 
*   (781) 359-7451 
*   (703) 725-5982 
*   (781) 359-7553 
*   (781) 917-5143 
*   (857) 416-3705 
*   (603) 493-4520 

  Any other phone number known by the department to be a phone contact for Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or Customs and Border Patrol (CBP). 

4. Text message records from department issued cell phones (including SMS, RCS, and iMessage) communications sent or received on department issued mobile devices from January 20, 2025, to the date this request is responded to, that contain any of the following terms: 

*       ICE 
*       CBP 
*       Immigration 
*       Immigrant 
*       Detainer 

This request includes messages stored on devices themselves, in any backup system, cloud storage, mobile device management platform, or held by any third-party vendor on behalf of the department. 

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as this request is in the public interest. I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available.  

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.  I am happy to discuss this request with your staff to help clarify any matters. 

 

Sincerely, 

Joshua Dankoff 
Director of Strategic Initaitives 
Citizens for Juvenile Justice 
joshuadankoff@cfjj.org  
(submitted via muckrock)

title: Community Preservation Committee meeting of December 7, 2023

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Community Preservation Committee meeting of December 7, 2023
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to M.G.L. c. 66 §10, I respectfully request the following public records from the Plymouth Community Preservation Committee (CPC), forwarded via the Selectmen's Office as needed:

1. The complete minutes (draft and final) of the Plymouth Community Preservation Committee meeting held on or about December 7, 2023.
2. The complete audio and/or video recording of that meeting.
3. The agenda packet distributed to CPC members in advance of that meeting.
4. All applications, supporting documents, and recommendation memoranda associated with any grant award to A.D. Makepeace Company or any subsidiary or affiliated entity considered at that meeting.
5. The full roll-call vote on any CPC recommendation regarding A.D. Makepeace at that meeting.

If any portion is exempt, please identify the specific exemption claimed and produce the segregable portions. Per M.G.L. c. 66 §10, please respond within 10 business days.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Edward Havey

Dear  Edward Havey : 

We have received your public Records request for Town of Plymouth. This record is being assigned to appropriate department. The department reviewer will review the request and create an effort estimate to create the response. Per state mandate, for Town of Plymouth, if the effort estimate is more than 2 hours, we will create a proposed cost estimate and will email such estimate to your attention. Once you approve the amount and send the payment, we will start working on your request. 

In case the estimate is less than 2 hours, we will proceed to work on the response and will send you the response once the work is completed. Please also note that state mandate allows us to complete and send the response within 10 business days. We will do our due diligence towards this effort. However, the due date may be extended for any of the following reasons: 
1.   Any clarification that is required from you towards this request will stop the clock. The clock will be resumed once the proper clarification is received. 
2.   Payment of estimated effort – The time it takes to receive the due amount will be added to the original due date. 
3.    If in the opinion of the reviewer it is determined that original due date cannot be met despite reasonable effort, the due date shall be extended based on reviewer’s judgment. You will be promptly notified in such case with revised due date. 
Thank you. 
Connor Anderson

title: Public Records Request — Pennsylvania Generative AI Records

communications.subject: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: Public Records Request — Pennsylvania Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 9, 2026

Open Records Officer
Commonwealth of Pennsylvania
Office of Administration
613 North Street
Harrisburg, PA 17120

Dear Open Records Officer,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to Pennsylvania's use of generative AI tools by state employees, including ChatGPT Enterprise and Microsoft Copilot Chat.

We are requesting the following records under 65 P.S. § 67.901:

1. All interaction logs from ChatGPT Enterprise, Microsoft Copilot Chat, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by Commonwealth employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from January 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing Commonwealth employee use of ChatGPT Enterprise, Microsoft Copilot Chat, or other generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics for ChatGPT Enterprise and Microsoft Copilot Chat showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

Pennsylvania conducted a first-in-the-nation pilot program in partnership with OpenAI, in which 175 employees from 14 agencies used ChatGPT Enterprise over the course of a year, reporting an average time savings of 95 minutes per day (see Shapiro Administration, "Lessons from Pennsylvania's Generative AI Pilot with ChatGPT," https://www.pa.gov/content/dam/copapwp-pagov/en/oa/documents/programs/information-technology/documents/openai-pilot-report-2025.pdf; see also City & State PA, "Shapiro says AI has 'real promise' as he unveils pilot program results," Mar. 2025, https://www.cityandstatepa.com/policy/2025/03/shapiro-says-ai-has-real-promise-he-unveils-pilot-program-results/403965/). The administration subsequently expanded access to ChatGPT Enterprise and Microsoft Copilot Chat to all qualified Commonwealth employees, with approximately 2,200 employees now on Copilot. Over 1,300 employees have completed InnovateUS training on safe AI use, with 3,200 more enrolled (see Spotlight PA, "Critics warn of risks as more PA governments turn to AI," Aug. 2025, https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/08/ai-chatgpt-shapiro-pennsylvania-government-environment/; see also PublicSource, "Pennsylvania government to expand employees' use of AI," https://www.publicsource.org/pennsylvania-government-expand-artificial-intelligence-ai/).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, "WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents" (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. For Google Gemini, interaction data may be available through Google Vault or Google Takeout. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Pennsylvania's Right-to-Know Law defines "record" as information regardless of physical form that documents a transaction or activity of an agency. 65 P.S. § 67.102. The Office of Open Records has confirmed that electronic communications on all platforms constitute public records. Governor Shapiro's Executive Order 2024-02 established a Generative AI Governance Board, and the Office of Information Technology issued GenAI guidelines directing state employees to document AI tool usage and treat AI-generated outputs as agency records. Consistent with this trend nationally, NARA has made clear that chats and other electronic messages used for agency business are likely government records. NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02.

communications.subject: RE: [External] Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: Public Records Request — Pennsylvania Generative AI Records  - OA RTKL Request: 2026.034
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Spangher:
On April 17, 2026, the open-records officer of the Office of Administration (OA) received your written request for information.  OA is responding to your request under the Pennsylvania’s Right-To-Know Law (RTKL), Act of February 14, 2008, P.L. 6, No.3, as amended, 65 P.S. §§ 67.101-67.3104.  Under the RTKL, a written response to your request is due on or before April 24, 2026.

This is an interim response, not a final response, to your requests.  Under the provisions of 65 P.S. § 67.902(b)(2), you are hereby notified that your request is being reviewed for the reason(s) listed below and OA will require up to an additional 30 days, i.e., until May 26, 2026, in which to respond to your request.

·  Compliance with your request may require the redaction of certain information that is not subject to access under RTKL.

·  Your request is under legal review to determine whether a requested record is a “public record” for purposes of the RTKL.

·  The extent or nature of the request precludes a response within the required time period.

Finally, the estimated or actual total for any fees that may be owed when the record becomes available will be included in our subsequent response.  Prepayment is required before providing access when the estimated cost to fulfill each request exceeds $100.00.  65 P.S. § 67.1307(h).  If you have any questions regarding this letter, please contact me.

Sincerely,



Wha Lee Strohecker
Agency Open Records Officer
Governor’s Office of Administration
110 Finance Building
Harrisburg, Pennsylvania 17120

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

The information you requested contains the following confidential information and/or information that is excepted from disclosure under section 552.021 of the Texas Government Code: [ x ] Normally, we must request a ruling from the Texas Attorney General before we can withhold some of the information you requested.  However, sections 552.136 (Account Access), 552.101 (Common Law-Confidential Information), 552.102 (Confidentiality of Certain Personnel Information), 552.137 (Confidentiality of Certain E-Mail Addresses), 159.002 (Medical Records), 552.147 (Social Security), 411.090 (Criminal History), 772.218/772 (Originating telephone numbers and addresses of 9-1-1 callers), 560.065 Transportation code (Accident Reports), 552.130 (Motor Vehicle), 552.127 (Crime Watch), 552.1085 (Confidentiality of Sensitive Crime Scene Image) llow us to withhold this specific information without requesting a ruling from the attorney general. We have also received previous determinations that allow us to withhold some of the requested information pursuant to Attorney General Open Records Decision No. 684 (2009) and Open Records Letter Ruling No. 2017-09757 (2017). You have the right to appeal our decision to withhold some of the information from you. Instructions for appeal are at the end of this letter.  If you do not want to appeal, you do not need to do anything else. We will process the rest of your request for information in accordance with the terms of the Public Information Act. Respectfully, DPD - Open Records Phone: 214-671-3148 How to appeal the withholding of information under Gov’t Code sections 552.024, 552.1175, 552.130, and 552.136: If you wish to appeal the withholding of information discussed on the previous page, you must send the following to the attorney general: 1) a signed, written statement indicating your wish to appeal the withholding of information; 2) the name of the governmental body that withheld information from you; 3) the date you made your original request for information; and 4) a copy of your original request for information, or if you are unable to provide a copy, a description of your original request for information. You may also submit written comments stating why you think the information should be released to you, but you are not required to do so. Send your appeal by mail to the attorney general at: Open Records Division P.O. Box 12548 Austin, Texas 78711-2548 Within forty-five business days after receiving all of the above-listed items necessary to file your appeal, the attorney general will issue a written ruling on the matter.  You will receive a copy of this ruling in the mail.

title: Use of force data collection (2017-2025) cities 50-100k (Mission Police Department)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Use of force data collection (2017-2025) cities 50-100k (Mission Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident location, area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: CPRA Request: Security camera procurement and policies (Carlmont High School/SUHSD)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: CPRA Request: Security camera procurement and policies (Carlmont High School/SUHSD)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Contracts and procurement records
All contracts, purchase orders, invoices, licensing agreements, Requests for Proposals (RFPs), and winning bid responses related to the acquisition, installation, maintenance, licensing, or upgrade of security camera systems at Carlmont High School or district-wide. I request these records for the period of January 1, 2018 through the date this request is fulfilled.

Please note that this request includes documents where Avigilon (including Motorola Solutions), Arecont Vision, or Axis Communications are the manufacturers of the hardware or software, even if the direct contract is with a third-party reseller, system integrator, or installation contractor (e.g., CDW-G, local security vendors, etc.).

Specifically, I seek records regarding:
Hardware: Avigilon H4 and H5A Multisensor series, Axis network cameras, Arecont Vision panoramic cameras, and Network Video Recorders (NVRs).
Software: Avigilon Control Center (ACC) software, "Appearance Search" feature licensing, facial recognition licenses, and any "Focus of Attention" interface licensing.

Procurement records are likely held by the SUHSD Purchasing Department or Administrative Services.

2. Internal and external communications
All email communications between SUHSD staff (specifically: Superintendent, Director of Maintenance & Operations, Director of Technology, Purchasing Managers, and school site administrators [Principals and Vice Principals]) and any representative with an email domain belonging to avigilon.com, motorolasolutions.com, arecontvision.com, or axis.com. I request these records for the period of January 1, 2020 through the date this request is fulfilled.

I also request all internal emails among SUHSD staff containing the specific terms: "Appearance Search," "Facial Recognition," "LPR," "License Plate Recognition," "AI Analytics," "Unusual Motion Detection," "Avigilon Cloud," or "Watch List" for the same time period.

Please note that I am not requesting technical configuration logs or password resets, but rather communications regarding the purchase decision, policy implementation, and capability assessments of these systems.

3. Policies and guidelines
Any written policies, guidelines, memoranda, directives, or Board Policies (BP) and Administrative Regulations (AR) adopted by SUHSD or any individual SUHSD campus governing:
The use of video surveillance on school grounds.
The retention schedule for security footage (how long data is kept before deletion).
Access protocols (who is authorized to view live or recorded footage).
The use of biometric analysis, facial recognition, or video analytics technology.
Any Data Privacy Agreements (DPAs) signed with security vendors regarding the processing of student images.

Policies are likely held by the Superintendent’s Office or Student Services.

To assist in determining my fee status, you should know that I am a student journalist writing for Scot Scoop and that this request is being made as part of news gathering for public distribution.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Maximus Kwan

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: CPRA Request: Security camera procurement and policies (Carlmont High School/SUHSD)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

This letter supplements my California Public Records Act request originally submitted to the Sequoia Union High School District on February 15, 2026, regarding security cameras. Through my reporting, I have identified Ojo Technology (acquired by Convergint Technologies, LLC in 2022) as the system integrator for SUHSD. 

I request the following additional records:

1. Contracts and Service Agreements
All contracts, service agreements, maintenance agreements, statements of work, Data Processing Agreements, Data Privacy Addenda, and amendments between SUHSD and Ojo Technology, Inc. (Fremont, CA) from January 1, 2018, to the present.

All contracts, service agreements, maintenance agreements, statements of work, Data Processing Agreements, Data Privacy Addenda, and amendments between SUHSD and Convergint Technologies, LLC from August 1, 2022, to the present.

2. Disclosures and Vendor Notices
Any vendor disclosures, subprocessor agreements, or data-sharing provisions governing the transfer of SUHSD security camera footage or associated data to third parties, including but not limited to Avigilon Corporation or Motorola Solutions, Inc.

Any communications from Ojo Technology or Convergint Technologies notifying SUHSD of the change in company ownership in 2022, and any SUHSD response to this notification.

3. External Communications
All email communications between the specific SUHSD staff identified in my original request (Superintendent, Director of Maintenance & Operations, Director of Technology, Purchasing Managers, and school site administrators [Principals and Vice Principals]) and any email address at the domains @ojotech.com or @convergint.com.

Please note that I am not requesting technical configuration logs or password resets, but rather communications regarding the purchase decision, policy implementation, and capability assessments of these systems.

To align with the communication scope of my original request, I request these emails for the period of January 1, 2020, through the date this request is fulfilled.


I request that SUHSD process this supplement concurrently with my original request. However, please do not delay the planned March 16, 2026, rolling production of my original requested documents to accommodate this supplement. If these new items require a separate production timeline, please inform me.

As previously stated, I am a student journalist writing for Scot Scoop. This request is made for news gathering, and the documents will be made available to the public. As such, I ask that any fees be waived. If fees are assessed, please notify me prior to fulfilling the request. I request all documents be provided in electronic format.

Thank you again for your time and assistance in processing this matter.

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: CPRA Request: Security camera procurement and policies (Carlmont High School/SUHSD)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act (Government Code § 7920.000 et seq.), I request copies of the following records. Where records exist in electronic form, I request them in their native electronic format. I request that responsive records be produced on a rolling basis as they are identified, rather than withheld until all records have been compiled.

1. A Google Slides presentation titled "Student Bathrooms on Campus-Camera," referenced in an April 8, 2021 email from Gregg Patner (Administrative Vice Principal, Carlmont High School) to Ricardo Alva (Chief Technology Officer, SUHSD) and Sergio Mendez. This document was attached to or linked within that email chain with the subject line "Re: Cameras at Carlmont."

2. Any worksheet, spreadsheet, shared document, or similar record described as a "Camera Priorities" document for Carlmont High School, referenced in the same April 7-8, 2021 email chain among Gregg Patner, Ricardo Alva, and Sergio Mendez. Ricardo Alva's April 7, 2021 email refers to "the items listed in your Camera Priorities documents." Gregg Patner's April 7, 2021 email describes this as "this worksheet created after the walk through yesterday" and states that editing rights were shared with Alva and Mendez.

3. Any map, diagram, floor plan, or annotated campus layout shared among Carlmont High School or SUHSD staff in connection with the April 2021 camera evaluation walkthrough, including but not limited to the map referenced in Ricardo Alva's April 8, 2021 email as "the map Gregg sent."

4. Any additional records (including emails, meeting notes, or shared documents) generated in connection with the April 6-8, 2021 camera evaluation walkthrough at Carlmont High School attended by Gregg Patner, Ricardo Alva, Sergio Mendez, and Mike Maddox of Avigilon.

I am a student journalist with Scot Scoop, the student newspaper of Carlmont High School within the Sequoia Union High School District. This request is made in the public interest to inform the school community about how district administrators and a private vendor planned and scoped the placement of surveillance cameras on the Carlmont campus, including coverage near student restrooms, prior to the formal award of design and installation contracts in May and June 2021. As such, I respectfully request a waiver or reduction of any fees associated with this request.

Because I am requesting electronic copies of these records in their native format, I expect there to be no fees associated with this request pursuant to Gov. Code § 7922.570. If the estimated cost of producing these records exceeds USD 50, please notify me before proceeding so that I may narrow the request if necessary.

I request a determination on disclosability within 10 calendar days as required by Government Code § 7922.535. If you determine that any portion of the requested records is exempt from disclosure, please redact only those portions and provide the remainder of the record. For any redaction or denial, please cite the specific legal exemption justifying the withholding, as required by Government Code § 7922.540.

Thank you for your attention to this request.

Sincerely,
Maximus Kwan

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Washington County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Arkansas Freedom of Information Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Washington County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arkansas Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Issaquah Business Loss Prevention Group

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Issaquah Business Loss Prevention Group
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

The February 25, 2025 city council agenda minutes (https://issaquah.civicweb.net/document/363590/) mention a partnership between the Issaquah Police Department and the "Issaquah Business Loss Prevention Group". Please provide the following records:
1. All contracts, MOUs, MOAs, NDAs, and other agreements between IPD and the Issaquah Business Loss Prevention Group
2. All emails and Microsoft Teams messages involving the Issaquah Business Loss Prevention Group between July 1, 2025 and March 1, 2026, including attachments and associated metadata.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: Police Records Request :: P023906-033026
communications.communication: SUCCESS! YOUR POLICE RECORDS REQUEST HAS BEEN SUBMITTED AND ASSIGNED THE REFERENCE #P023906-033026. We will contact you by 4/6/2026 regarding the status of your request.  If you not receive a response by this date, please email IPDRequests@issaquahwa.gov or call 425-837-3200. (mailto:IPDRequests@issaquahwa.gov) It is our goal to provide you with the records you are seeking as quickly as possible.  Please note that it may take us some time to locate, assemble, and review records prior to their release to you.  We will try to release records in installments as they become available.  The first installment of records is typically available within 2-4 weeks. We will keep you updated on the status of your request and any fees that may apply.  Please keep an eye out for emails or phone calls from us about your request and respond to any questions we may have at your earliest convenience. Your request will be processed in the order it was received, and alongside all the other requests for records.  If you have multiple records requests currently open with us, we will work on them one at a time in the order they were received unless you specify which request you would like processed first. Please note that copies of collision reports are $8.00.  The same report may be available for less through the Washington State Patrol's website or  BuyCrash.com.  You may contact us at any time regarding your request. Issaquah Police Records Unit Issaquah Police Records Portal  425-837-3200 You can monitor the progress of your request in " My Records Center".

If payment is not received by June 1, 2026, and you have not contacted us to make alternate arrangements, your request will be considered abandoned and it will be closed, with no further records provided per Section 3.8 of our records request policy. Pay Online with a Credit or Debit card: Visa and Mastercard payments are accepted.  A non-refundable 3% service fee applies. To pay online: 1) Log in to your records account.  2) Select View My Invoice(s). 3) Select the Make a Payment button for request #P023906-033026. 4) Read the instructions and click the Submit button. Pay with Cash (exact change only) , Check, or Money Order: There are no service fees for these options.  Please let us know if you plan to pay in person or by mail so we can provide you with more information. Once payment is received, the records will typically be made available to you within 1 business day.  Note that payments made after 5:00 pm will be received on the following business day.  Please reply to this email or call the number below if you have any questions or require any assistance. Best regards, Hannah Haver Public Records Analyst - City Clerk's Office City of Issaquah, WA 425-837-3027 To monitor the progress or update this request please log into the Issaquah Public Records Center

title: BlueLeaks (Department of Homeland Security)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: BlueLeaks (Department of Homeland Security)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Documents mentioning, describing or generated in response to the BlueLeaks release, the preceding hack or subsequent fallout, including but not limited to:

* Damage assessments
* Emails
* Interagency communications (local, state, or federal)
* Communications with the press about BlueLeaks
* Communications with Twitter or other social media or sharing platforms

2. Documents mentioning or describing Distributed Denial of Secrets (DDoSecrets)

You may limit this request to records generated between November 1, 2018 and the present.

I am a member of the news media and request classification as such. I have previously written about the government and its activities, with some reaching over 100,000 readers in outlets such as Gizmodo, MuckRock, Motherboard, Property of the People, Unicorn Riot, and The Outline, among others. As such, as I have a reasonable expectation of publication and my editorial and writing skills are well established. In addition, I discuss and comment on the files online and make them available through non-profits such as the library Internet Archive and the journalist non-profit MuckRock, disseminating them to a large audience. While my research is not limited to this, a great deal of it, including this, focuses on the activities and attitudes of the government itself. As such, it is not necessary for me to demonstrate the relevance of this particular subject in advance.

As my primary purpose is to inform about government activities by reporting on it and making the raw data available, I request that fees be waived.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Emma Best

title: Leadership and constituent emails (Dover School District/SAU11)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Dover School District/SAU11)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by each school board chair in the district/SAU related to their position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on district or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

communications.subject: Fwd: 26-1466 - IAD
communications.communication:
*RE:                        PIA #: 26-1466*



*Requesting:        1. All reports related to Use of Force (UoF) incidents
created since March 3, 2026*



Dear Requester:



On April 3, 2026, the Maryland State Police received your request under the
Public Information Act (Annotated Code of Maryland, General Provisions
Article § 4-101 through §4-601) for the information captioned above.



I am writing to advise that it will take us more than 10 working days to
process the requested record(s). The delay is due to the time required to
perform a thorough search and conduct the necessary review process. Once
the record(s) has been collected, it must be reviewed to determine whether
it is, in whole or in part, exempt from disclosure under the Act.  If any
information is to be withheld, you will be provided an explanation as to
why it cannot be released.  As soon as records are identified, you will be
notified of any fees that may apply.



We believe that we will be able to respond to your request within 30 days
of the date it was received.  However, if we can complete your request more
quickly, you will receive a response sooner. Please note that all records
would only pertain to the involvement of the Maryland State Police.



Please do not hesitate to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
about your request.


Sincerely

communications.subject: Fwd: 26-1466 - IAD
communications.communication:
*RE:                   PIA #: 26-1466*



*Requesting:    1. All reports related to Use of Force (UoF) incidents
created since March 3, 2026*



Dear Requester:



On April 3, 2026, the Maryland State Police received your request under the
Public Information Act (Annotated Code of Maryland, General Provisions
Article §§ 4-101 through 4-601) for the information captioned above.
Accordingly, the requested records are attached to this email.



Please note the following redactions that have been applied:

Ø  Medical Information has been redacted pursuant to GP§ 4-329

Ø  Sociological information has been redacted pursuant to GP§ 4-330

Ø  Personal Privacy information has been redacted pursuant to GP §
4-351(a)(4)



Pursuant to GP § 4-362, you are entitled to seek judicial review of this
decision. You also have the option to file a complaint with the Public
Information Act Compliance Board concerning the amount of the fee charged, *see
*GP § 4-1A-01 *et seq.*, and may also refer any concerns about this decision
to the Public Access Ombudsman pursuant to GP § 4-1B-01 *et seq.*



Having responded to your request, the Maryland State Police now considers
this request to be closed.

Sincerely,

Heather King

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Heather King <heather.king@maryland.gov>
Date: Tue, May 19, 2026 at 8:37 AM
Subject: Fwd: 26-1466 - IAD
To: <208177-38116506@requests.muckrock.com>
Cc: MSP MEDIA -State Police- <msp.media@maryland.gov>


*RE:                        PIA #: 26-1466*



*Requesting:        1. All reports related to Use of Force (UoF) incidents
created since March 3, 2026*



Dear Requester:



This letter is to inform you that the Maryland State Police has located
records that pertain to your request submitted under the Public Information
Act (Annotated Code of Maryland, General Provisions Article §§ 4-101
through 4-601).  The records in question include the following:



*1. All reports related to Use of Force (UoF) incidents created since March
3, 2026*



Note that the Maryland State Police charges a fee for copies of records at
the rate of $0.25 per page, $15.00 per audio recording, $25.00 per video
recording.  There is also a fee charged for the search, collection, and
redaction of all applicable information at a rate equal to the hourly pay
of the employee(s) working to fulfill the request (first two hours gratis).



*If you will accept your request in an electronic format the fee will be
waived. **Please confirm whether you will accept the electronic format or
prefer to receive the files via USPS Certified Mail. **Upon receipt of your
decision, a statement or your file(s) will be sent.*



Do not hesitate to contact me with any questions or concerns.



Sincerely,

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Heather King <heather.king@maryland.gov>
Date: Tue, Apr 14, 2026 at 3:11 PM
Subject: Fwd: 26-1466 - IAD
To: <208177-38116506@requests.muckrock.com>


*RE:                        PIA #: 26-1466*



*Requesting:        1. All reports related to Use of Force (UoF) incidents
created since March 3, 2026*



Dear Requester:



On April 3, 2026, the Maryland State Police received your request under the
Public Information Act (Annotated Code of Maryland, General Provisions
Article § 4-101 through §4-601) for the information captioned above.



I am writing to advise that it will take us more than 10 working days to
process the requested record(s). The delay is due to the time required to
perform a thorough search and conduct the necessary review process. Once
the record(s) has been collected, it must be reviewed to determine whether
it is, in whole or in part, exempt from disclosure under the Act.  If any
information is to be withheld, you will be provided an explanation as to
why it cannot be released.  As soon as records are identified, you will be
notified of any fees that may apply.



We believe that we will be able to respond to your request within 30 days
of the date it was received.  However, if we can complete your request more
quickly, you will receive a response sooner. Please note that all records
would only pertain to the involvement of the Maryland State Police.



Please do not hesitate to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
about your request.


Sincerely

title: FOIA Request: Policy 430 - Automated License Plate Readers (ALPR)

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: FOIA Request: Policy 430 - Automated License Plate Readers (ALPR)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

According to Forest Park Police Department Policy 430, with reference to 625 ILCS 5/2-130, it is stated

- Section III / F: "The Chief of Police or their designee, serves as the ALPR Administrator for the agency"
- Section IV Procedures:
A: "No member of this department shall operate ALPR equipment or access ALPR data without first completing department-approved training"
D: "ALPR Administrator shall monitor to ensure the security of the information and compliance with applicable privacy laws"
G: "If an ALPR is used...a Street Camera / LPR Use Form must be completed and forwarded to the Patrol Lieutenant who will keep a record of all uses"
- Section I Investigative Procedures
Q1: "ALPR system audits should be conducted on a regular basis"
Q2: "The Chief or their designee should download and save monthly ALPR system User and Access data for at least two years and one month"

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records

The documents I am seeking include, but are not limited to:
1) Any document recording the name of the Forest Park ALPR Administrator, if this role is not fulfilled by the Chief of Police.

2) Training Materials: The department-approved training materials used as a prerequisite for sworn officers to use or access ALPR data as per section IV - A

3) Training Records: Certificates of completion or other records showing sworn officer completion of the training provided to staff as per section IV - A

4) Policies, procedures and records of how the ALPR Administrator monitors to ensure the security of the ALPR information to ensure compliance with applicable privacy laws as per section IV - D

5) Copies of the Street Camera / LPR Use Forms from September 2025 to present as per section IV - G

6) Policies, procedures and records of the ALPR system audits conducted per section Q of Policy 430

7) Monthly ALPR system User and Access data for the last two years and one month as per section Q


The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

ll

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

- All contracts, licenses, MOUs, MOAs, data agreements, letters of intent, State of Work documents, Scope of Work documents, and other agreement documents with Convergint Technologies that were active any time between 1/1/2022 and 11/11/2025.

Sincerely,

e

title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request
communications.communication:
Dear Open Records FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Open Records county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request #26-791
communications.communication:
Hello Ms. Ambrose,

I'm writing directly because the Calhoun FOIA portal email has been unresponsive to my recent replies. I submitted a FOIA request 3/26/2026 (tracking #26-791) for the Calhoun County assessment roll — SEV, taxable value, PRE status, classification, school district, owner name/address. I believe the responsive records are maintained by the Equalization Department under MCL 211.34 / 211.34d.

Penny in the Treasurer's Office responded on 3/27/2026 asking which 'tax roll' (2025 or delinquent) I was requesting, and quoted $0.30/parcel capped at $2,000 under what appears to be the MCL 48.101 fee framework. But I'm not requesting Treasurer records — my request is for Equalization Department assessment data. Could you route the request to the Equalization Department and ask them to provide a FOIA-compliant itemized estimate under MCL 15.234 for the assessment roll they have?

If it's easier on your end,  sending those records via MuckRock ensure they are publicly searchable.

If you cannot release the responsive records, please respond so I can initiate an appeal under MCL15.240 ( https://www.legislature.mi.gov/Laws/MCL?objectName=mcl-15-240 ) next.

Thanks,
Alex
awmunro@gmail.com · 734-272-6612
34 N Huron St OFC, Ypsilanti MI 48197

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request #26-791
communications.communication:
Hello!

I see I didn't get an answer to my earlier questions-- would it be easier to explain via phone? 734-272-6612 if you could give me a call.

I had seen the FOIA, its cost limitations, FOIA's list of exemptions (many items are exempt but I didn't see assessment roll among exemptions), and I had heard about Grebner v Clinton Twp (where I believe the courts ruled that public records should be avail under an incremental-cost basis) -- so I am hoping to understand why the responsive records wouldn't be released under FOIA's invoicing and cost limitations; I'm willing to pay if needed, I just don't want to overpay.

Or how would you square this?

Thank you,

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request #26-791
communications.communication:
Hi Leissa, Kate and Penny,

Just to clarify; I would like 2018 thru 2025 (and 2026 when it's ready). Would that make the total $20,000 for Calhoun to gather some exported .csv files from BS&A for me? I would like clarity on the total for appeal. And can you confirm whether I am being charged under the EAPRA or under the TARA, if not under the FOIA for my FOIA request?

As I look back at our correspondence, I'm afraid that my request seems to have been shopped between different departments in order to find a statutory basis with which to charge the most money for my request's fulfilment. This is disappointing, but I would like to understand the situation and so I am making a new request for records of a different category than the assessment tax records I've been denied. Please provide:

1) Data-sharing agreements, licenses, or MOUs between Calhoun County and any of: Zillow Group, Redfin, Move Inc./Realtor.com, CoreLogic, ATTOM Data Solutions, Black Knight/ICE Mortgage Technology, First American Financial, Old Republic, Stewart Title, Fidelity National Financial, LexisNexis Risk Solutions, DataTree, PropertyRadar, Constellation Software/BS&A, Michigan REALTORS, MiRealSource, RealComp. Or if Calhoun is running their data sales on-the-fly via email, please confirm in writing.

2) Email correspondence between Calhoun County employees (Equalization, Treasurer, Information Systems, Administrator's offices) and representatives of the entities in #1 regarding assessment data, parcel data, or sales data. If no such emails exist, please confirm in writing.

3) Invoices issued by Calhoun County to any of the entities in #1. If no such records exist, please confirm in writing.

4) Records of payments received by Calhoun County from any of the entities in #1 -- often this is in an Excel-friendly format like the data I requested. If no such records exist, please confirm in writing.

5) Transmittal records (cover emails, ShareFile or FTP logs, mailing receipts) showing what files were transmitted to any of the entities in #1 -- this is to help me understand how you transmit that to the entities paying $2500 per year.

Thanks,

Alexander Munro
34 N Huron St OFC
Ypsilanti, MI 48197
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com -- personal email

PS: This was sent via MuckRock so this request is on the public record. MuckRock is a publicly searchable open-records platform commonly used by journalists and citizens; it's not used by commercial data brokers (Zillow, CoreLogic, etc.) for routine data acquisitions. If you'd like your response to be publicly searchable as well, you can CC the MuckRock when you reply. This request should be processed under FOIA's MCL 15.234 actual-cost framework rather than under any commercial fee schedule.

communications.subject: [External Message Added] Calhoun County, MI public records request #26-791
communications.communication:
Calhoun County, MI ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-791: Dear Requester: Calhoun County has received your request for public records under the Michigan Freedom of Information Act (FOIA). My goal with this message it to clear up any confusion that may exist. The assessment information that you are requesting is not created by a County Department. Each individual local municipality creates their assessment data, which is their opinion of assessed values. The local municipalities submit their data to the County Equalization Department for use in the County Equalization's determination of action. Therefore, the information you have requested is exempt from release under MCL 15.243(1)(m). However, the information you are seeking is publicly available on the Equalization Department's website using this link: Home | Calhoun County | BS&A Online When your request was initially received, it was assigned to the Treasurer's Office to fulfill. The initial response from the Treasurer's Office was that for that office to perform the research on your behalf, the cost would be $.30 per parcel look-up with a maximum of $2000. That amount is set pursuant to MCL 48.101. Your initial request should have been closed with a more-detailed explanation. Your request was then reopened and assigned to the Equalization Department, as the Treasurer's Office believed you were seeking 2025 data, that has not yet been completed, and the response was that the records for 2025 do not yet exist. The Equalization Departments states on their website that they charge $2000/2500 to compile the information requested, which again is publicly available on their website. Your request will remain closed because the information you seek regarding each municipalities' assessed value is exempt from disclosure under MCL 15.243(1)(m). You can request the information directly from each municipality. The County's Equalization Report, which is the final agency determination, is available using the following website link: Calhoun County, MI Sincerely, Kate Ambrose Corporation Counsel/FOIA Coordinator ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at Calhoun County, MI.

If any fields are exempt under MCL 15.243, please redact those portions and produce the remainder per MCL 15.244. I'd appreciate the request being interpreted liberally consistent with MCL 15.231(2).

Per MCL 15.234, I'd appreciate advance notice if the estimated fee exceeds $50, with itemization. If a phone call would help clarify scope, I'm at 734-272-6612.

Thank you,
Alex Munro
34 N Huron St OFC, Ypsilanti, MI 48197
734-272-6612

title: Peter J. Peters

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Peter J. Peters
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records relating to, mentioning, or generated as a result of investigation into Peter John Peters (November 13, 1946 – July 7, 2011), who led the LaPorte Church of Christ in Laporte, Colorado until his death. Peters used the church to promote white supremacist ideology. Proof of death is not required as his death was widely reported: https://www.splcenter.org/hatewatch/2011/07/15/influential-christian-identity-pastor-dies

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records for all relevant names, individuals, agencies and companies. This search is also to include any responsive records held in offsite locations, such as the Alexandria Records Center. Please include both investigatory and non-investigatory records in your search. If any potentially responsive records have been destroyed and/or transferred to NARA, then I request copies of the destruction or transfer slips as well as any other documentation relating to, mentioning or describing said transfer or destruction, to include but not be limited to confirmation that the Bureau has no other copies of said records. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Paul Galante
